title: FIXFlux - Distributed Traces
description: Saved TraceQL queries for tracing single orders across the order-service → risk-service → matching-engine → trade-store pipeline

panels.title: Full Order Lifecycle (order-service → trade-store)
panels.description:
Traces that span the complete happy path: order received, risk-approved, matched, and persisted as a trade.

Each row is one order. Click a row to open the full span waterfall — you will see timing for each service hop.

TraceQL: `{name="order-service.process"} >> {name="trade-store.persist"}`

panels.title: Risk-Approved Orders Only
panels.description:
Traces where risk-service forwarded the order downstream (approved). Structural query: order-service span must be followed by a matching-engine span — rejected orders never reach the matching engine.

TraceQL: `{name="order-service.process"} >> {name="matching-engine.process"}`

panels.title: Risk-Rejected Orders Only
panels.description:
Traces where the order entered risk-service but never reached the matching engine — these are blocked orders.

The risk-service span topic will be `risk_rejected_orders`. Use this to investigate which risk rule fired.

TraceQL: `{name="order-service.process"} !>> {name="matching-engine.process"}`

panels.title: Slow Matching Engine Spans (> 50 ms)
panels.description:
Matching-engine spans that took longer than 50 ms to process an order — useful for spotting order book contention or GC pauses under load.

Adjust the threshold in the query editor to tighten or loosen the filter.

TraceQL: `{name="matching-engine.process" && duration > 50ms}`

panels.title: Slowest End-to-End Traces (all services)
panels.description:
The 20 slowest traces across all instrumented services, sorted by duration. Useful for finding outliers during load tests or E2E runs.

TraceQL: `{resource.service.name=~"order-service|risk-service|matching-engine|trade-store"} | select(duration)` sorted by duration descending.

panels.title: Error Spans (any service)
panels.description:
Any span across all services that finished with status=error. In normal operation this panel should be empty — if rows appear, click through to see the stack trace attached to the failing span.

TraceQL: `{status=error}`
